layman_headline: The market is bleeding red — patience is the smartest play right now.
layman_explanation: Nearly every cryptocurrency is down today, with Bitcoin dropping 3% and many altcoins falling much harder. The Fear & Greed index sits at 28, meaning most investors are scared. While fear can eventually create buying opportunities, none of the candidates on the watchlist present a clean, high-confidence setup with good risk/reward right now. The best move is to wait for the selling to exhaust itself and pounce when a clear setup emerges.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. Do not chase falling knives. A better entry point with clearer signals should emerge in the coming days as the market stabilizes.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate was evaluated and found lacking: LIT has strong 5-day momentum (+55.8%) but RSI at 72.35 signals overbought conditions with a 12.5% single-day drop — classic blow-off top risk. GRASS dropped 31% in 7 days but shows a bearish engulfing candle with chart bias at -0.90 and risk/reward of only 0.48x. VELVET surged 22% today but the chart bias is slightly bearish (-0.30) with terrible risk/reward (0.26x) near resistance. MON's risk/reward is 0.46x with price below all MAs. BTC dominance at 55.9% continues to pressure altcoins. The Fear & Greed improving trend (24.6 weekly avg → 28 today) suggests we're approaching a capitulation bottom, but we're not there yet. Best strategy is to wait for confirmed reversal signals.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation (Trump/Hormuz Strait) could trigger further risk-asset selloffs
BTC breaking below $60K would likely drag all alts down another 10-20%
High BTC dominance (55.9%) means capital is rotating out of alts — fighting the trend
watch_list: BTC price action around $60K-$62K support — a bounce here could signal the bottom
Fear & Greed index crossing above 30 as a sign of sentiment stabilization
GRASS at $0.30 support level — if it holds after a 31% weekly drop, could be a strong mean-reversion play
market_context: Broad crypto selloff with BTC down 3% threatening $62K, driven by geopolitical fears around the Hormuz Strait. Fear & Greed at 28 is improving from weekly average of 24.6, suggesting we may be approaching peak fear but haven't confirmed a bottom yet.
context_banner: Market-wide fear selloff with no clean altcoin setups — disciplined patience will be rewarded when capitulation completes.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from weekly avg of 24.6 — approaching potential capitulation zone but not yet confirmed as a bottom.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear — suggests we're nearing exhaustion but BTC still dropping 3% today.
Bitcoin threatens $62K amid geopolitical risk as Trump discusses controlling Hormuz Strait — adding macro uncertainty to an already weak market.
No candidate has RSI in deep oversold territory (<30); LIT is actually overbought at 72.35, making it dangerous to enter despite the pullback.
GRASS bearish engulfing (-0.90 bias), VELVET bearish marubozu (-0.30 bias), MON/XPL below key MAs — no chart is giving a clear buy signal with favorable risk/reward.
